solutions.solution: Complete check to our sample problem Quick check: Both events are in "present perfect" tense. So, it can't be definitely said which one is antecedent to which one. Next, we check for any causal connections. We conclude that there doesn't seem to be any possible link between India winning prizes in essays and rise in prices of gold in the local market. Correct choice: 5

Quick check: Event A is in "present perfect" tense and event B is in "past" tense. So, A is antecedent to B, this means B can't be a cause of A and this eliminates choices 1 and 3. Next, we check for any causal connection. Apparently, A can cause B. Now, is A the immediate and principal cause? Yes. It is immediate because B follows A very soon and it is principal because had A not occurred, B would not have followed. Correct choice: 2

Quick check: Event A is in "present perfect" and event B is in "present continuous". So, A occurs before B. This eliminates choices 1 and 3 and B can't be a cause. Now we check if A can be a cause. Apparently, A can cause B, in the sense that low prices may result in more purchases and more purchases may result in more computers per school and, therefore, it may result in more children showing interest. Obviously, it is a long chain and although A can be said to cause B somehow, A can't be said to be an immediate cause of B. Correct choice: 4

Quick check: Event A is in "present perfect" tense and B is present continuous. So, A occurs before B and hence B can't be a cause. This eliminates choices 1 and 3. Now, can A cause B? No. Bank M is celebrating its silver jubilee. (Remember, You are not to assume anything beyond this information), and merely this does not provide any cause for getting more customers. Do you open an account in a bank only because the bank is celebrating its silver jubilee? Correct choice: 5

Quick check: Both A and B in present perfect tense. So, which is antecedent to which, can't be declared by mere glance. Next, we check for any causal links. Apparently, B can cause A. In fact, B is the immediate and principal cause behind A because the opening of the flyover has a direct bearing on the traffic. Correct choice: 2
